title: Dysphania pusilla
taxonomy: Dysphania pusilla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a member of the genus Dysphania, which is a group of annual or biennial plants. Dysphania pusilla is also known as tiny saltbush, and it is native to Australia. This species is further classified as a type of succulent, adapted to survive in dry environments. Dysphania pusilla is often found growing in salt-affected areas, where it has developed unique characteristics to cope with high salt levels.

intro: Dysphania pusilla, also known as small glasswort or sea purslane, is a low-growing succulent annual plant that thrives in coastal areas and is often found in salt marshes and beaches. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and infections.
